claim: Five OCM daemon instances on five Multipass VMs (Ubuntu 24.04, 2 vCPU / 2GB each) on a single host machine successfully discover each other within 30 seconds of last-node startup AND route a chat request through a 2-hop path (client -> peer A -> peer B -> inference -> response) with end-to-end latency under 500ms median across 20 trials.
source_for_claim: Internal hypothesis. iroh QUIC NAT-traversal residential success rate is reported ~90% (vendor) / ~80%+ (independent estimates); on a single-host VM fleet (no NAT, all on the same hypervisor bridge) success rate should be effectively 100% and discovery should be sub-30s on libp2p mDNS / iroh bootstrap.
comparison_anchor: single-process-noop-routing-baseline (in-process call without going over the loopback bridge — should be sub-millisecond; gap quantifies VM/transport overhead)
decision_rule: If CONFIRMED, the mesh transport (Phase 7+) is correctly tuned for residential / single-host conditions. If REFUTED on discovery, audit bootstrap configuration. If REFUTED on 2-hop latency, audit per-hop overhead (TCP stack, encryption, queue depth). If INCONCLUSIVE, expand from 5 to 10 nodes for variance reduction.
blocked_on: Phase 7+ mesh transport (libp2p/iroh) not yet implemented
Multipass harness wrapper not yet written (planned: bench/harness/multipass_fleet.py)
